_README: Interpretive text for the study-1 public leaderboard page. THESE ARE CLAIMS ABOUT RESULTS — they are only true of the study-1 dev runs (config=frozen, dataset=study-1/datasets/dev). study-1 dropped difficulty tiers, has a near-empty fabrication split (4 unanswerable questions / 3 facts — too small for a rate), and reports fresh accuracy at the FACT level (variants aggregated per fact_id). export_site.py cross-checks the gate_failures roster against models-gatefail.txt. If a re-run changes the picture, rewrite this file.

headline_findings.label: Never got the tools
headline_findings.detail: Five of the nine packages we tried never received the search tools at all — the GGUF chat template dropped the tool schema, so they scored zero searches and are off the board.

headline_findings.label: A 1-bit 27B holds up
headline_findings.detail: Bonsai-27B at a 1-bit quant (3.8 GB) lands in the top cluster on fresh accuracy — a genuinely surprising result for that bit-width.

headline_findings.label: The top five tie
headline_findings.detail: The top five models are one statistical cluster: every 90% CI overlaps the leader's. At 80 facts this ranks tiers, not fine positions.

headline_findings.label: The 2B keeps up with the 4B
headline_findings.detail: Qwen3.5-2B (1.3 GB) sits inside the top cluster with its own 4B sibling — half the file size, statistically indistinguishable here.

config_notes.provider: Brave. The frozen config's search backend.
config_notes.tool_desc: Enriched tool descriptions: tell the model what the tool is for and to write short keyword queries.
config_notes.system_prompt: Shipped prompt with the current date injected ('Today's date is X').
config_notes.max_turns: A five-turn agentic budget; the passers average well under two turns per question.
config_notes.enable_thinking: Off. Reasoning mode is disabled so every model is compared on the same footing.

metric_definitions.label: Fresh ✓
metric_definitions.description: Primary score. Share of FACTS answered correctly on the fresh (retrieval-required) split. Each fact has several phrasing variants; a fact's score is the mean correctness across its variants, and the reported rate is the mean over the 80 fresh facts. The 90% CI is a Wilson interval over 80 facts. These questions are about events after the models' training cutoff, so they cannot be answered from memory.

metric_definitions.label: Stable ✓
metric_definitions.description: Share of timeless general-knowledge facts answered correctly (same fact-level aggregation). Checks that adding web search does not damage what a model already knew.

metric_definitions.label: Searched
metric_definitions.description: Fraction of fresh questions on which the model actually issued at least one search. The dominant failure mode for small models is not searching at all.

metric_definitions.label: File size
metric_definitions.description: On-disk size of the exact GGUF weights file run, in decimal GB. This is the download and storage cost on a phone, and the axis of the accuracy-vs-size Pareto view.

gate_failures.title: Five packages never received the search tools
gate_failures.mechanism: These five recorded schema_not_rendered on all 399 fresh questions and issued zero searches. The llama.cpp/GGUF tool-calling path test-renders each model's chat template to decide whether it supports tools; for these packages the template ignores the tool block, so the search schema is silently left out of the prompt. schema_not_rendered is a canary for exactly this: the tools never landed, so the model could not search. This is a packaging/template failure, NOT a statement about the models' capability — they were never offered the tools. They are listed, not ranked.
gate_failures.takeaway: Gate on rendered capability, never a model allowlist: pass the tools, then assert the schema actually rendered. One check (schema_not_rendered == 0) catches all five and any future repack that drops the tool template.

limitations: Dev split only: 80 fresh facts (399 phrasing variants). Neighbouring models sit within noise; separating the top cluster needs roughly 400 facts, and the dataset is growable.
No fabrication claims: the unanswerable (hallucination-under-failure) split has only 4 questions across 3 facts here — too small to state a fabrication rate. It is pool-limited by generation.
Composition skew is declared: the fact pool leans sports/tech/business and is June-2026-heavy. A model tuned on other topics or languages could rank differently.
Scope is crisp-fact retrieval — short, checkable answers. It does not measure long-form synthesis, multi-hop reasoning depth, or reading comprehension beyond what a snippet or one page yields.
The sealed holdout (58 facts) is unused: the config was frozen before any dev run, so dev is itself the out-of-sample test. The holdout is reserved for a single future confirmation run.

Correctness is graded by an LLM judge (deepseek-v4-flash) against human-curated golds; the gold panel (gpt-5.6-luna + z-ai/glm-5.2) is disjoint from the judge. It is a strong signal, not a human grader.
Quality is measured on a workstation running the same quantised GGUF files a phone would run. Battery, heat and on-device latency are not measured.
bonsai-27b-q1 runs at 1-bit; the others are Q4_K_M except Qwen3-1.7B (Q6_K). Bit-width is a real difference in the weights.
cluster_note: This board resolves tiers, not fine positions. The top five (0.80–0.87) are one statistical cluster — all CIs overlap the leader's — so read the groupings, not the exact order.
pareto_note: The accuracy-vs-file-size view shows which models are not beaten on both axes at once. A model is on the frontier if no other model is at least as accurate AND at least as small.
gate_failure_takeaway: Gate on rendered capability, never a model allowlist. schema_not_rendered==0 is the single assertion that separates a real result from a package that never got the tools.
